judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent evidence; comparable overall, with Report 1 marginally more decisive on the AI-footer removal while Report 2 defers it to the doc owner.

report_1.evidence: "Export your visit history" (lines 22–26) — False as stated. ... There is no PDF-generation code anywhere in the repo. ... This entire section describes a feature that doesn't exist yet and must be removed. ... `app/security/config.py` reads: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... The doc claims the opposite ("now enabled for every clinic by default"). ... Must be corrected or removed before sending.

report_2.evidence: There is no export route, and `captures.json` itself flags the corresponding image as ... This entire section describes a feature that doesn't exist in the codebase and must be removed ... Directly contradicted by `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py`: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... This is the opposite of what the document claims and must be corrected or removed.

report_1.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a completely different, unrelated page. ... `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`. Whoever assembled the capture set grabbed the wrong screenshot for the "after" slot.

report_2.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen. The "after" half of this comparison does not show the scheduling calendar at all. ... "after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", "state": "seed-demo"}

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` is referenced in the doc but does not exist anywhere in the repository.** ... **`unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is not referenced by the document at all.**

report_2.evidence: | `img/after_export.png` | **No** | Referenced at line 26 of the doc but the file does not exist in `img/` ... Additionally, `img/unused_capture_07.png` **exists on disk but is not referenced anywhere in the document**

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** — this reads as an internal drafting note/disclaimer, not customer-facing copy. It should be stripped regardless of the accuracy issues above; it doesn't belong in an external clinic-facing communication.

report_2.evidence: **The footer line "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (line 33) reads as an internal production note that leaked into customer-facing copy — the word "draft" in particular is not something you'd want a clinic customer to read in the email they receive.
